rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography
Register match
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 9: 'the model's answer may not be reproducible' — direct, precise peer register throughout

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique
rules_evaluated.evidence: Self-consistency is a standard pattern name; no originator credit obligation detected

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer terms (Hallucination benchmark, detector panel) stay below cut; body uses plain equivalents

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 60: 'The take-home move is not always run self-consistency. The take-home move is: benchmark the check before you trust it.' — teaching move, not positioning defense

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Author-we ban — first-person-plural extension
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose
Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: Self-consistency, grounded, drift, evidence surface, detector — all earned in opening contrast or used in module context

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban
No em-dashes. Simple sentences
Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something
rules_evaluated.evidence: Headers: 'Prompt: regenerate', 'Prompt: compare', 'Prompt: reuse' — all action verbs

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename
rules_evaluated.evidence: No inline links in body prose; references to exercises use natural phrasing

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use
rules_evaluated.evidence: No collision-prone terms triggered (memory, skill, rule, plan, agent used in non-colliding contexts)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 5: 'the agent writes' (autonomous action), Line 12: 'The trainer can demo' (not addressing agent), Line 9: 'the model's answer' (LLM thinking) — correct split

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow
rules_evaluated.evidence: No meta-announcements or contemplative-pause prefixes; teaching moves stay in action

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: No [BRACKETS] placeholders; paths are deterministic; student must supply no slots

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 15: 'Spawn one subagent...' (lead-in implied by 'Prompt: regenerate'), Line 35: 'Compare the first and second briefing' (clear action), Line 62: 'I have output I want to quality-control...' (semantic lead)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block
rules_evaluated.evidence: All three prompts follow: bold header (Prompt: ...), destination chip *(Claude Code)*, blank line, fenced block

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path
rules_evaluated.evidence: No skill invocations present; subagent task tool used correctly without path leak

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 20-27: back-reference names first briefing as 'the file you just created'; line 42 reads both runs by deterministic path

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chained-prompt isolation — don't leak the downstream pipeline
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt 2 produces the demo without assuming downstream runs; Prompt 3 stands alone as reusable pattern

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-sample methods need structural enforcement in the prompt
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 15-32: self-consistency enforced via isolation (subagent reads ONLY fresh evidence, NOT cached outputs); two independent runs explicit

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompts use plain bullets and numbers; no internal **bold** or *italic* noise

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Citation discipline — extend cite-or-flag scope, and quote the supporting sentence
rules_evaluated.evidence: No citations in body or prompts; no fabrication risk or conventional-wisdom claims needing flags

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompt blocks require maintainer approval before edit
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three stable prompts; no pending edits flagged; ready for approval

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 30, 43, 74 all name `module-5/<filename>` and `judges/<filename>` — relative subpaths from working directory

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture contrasts groundedness (certainty) vs. reproducibility (nuance) — leaves room in unease; primes next module's shift
